title: centaurea alba var. cinerascens
intro: Centaurea alba var. cinerascens, also known as White Knapweed, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and other bioactive compounds, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Centaurea alba var. cinerascens are believed to provide various health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of cardiovascular health, and potential antioxidant effects. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its potential health benefits.
